behaviors.description: The content passed to the summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff: three source clients -> aggregation layer -> summary-generator input payload. A unit test of a single formatter would miss wiring/omission bugs. Integration-brake check: (a) no real OS/network behavior is required to reproduce this specific failure; in-process fakes for the three sources are sufficient, so not System. (b) assertion is about input payload completeness, not model output quality, so not Agentic. (c) no multi-agent sequence, so not Workflow. Rejection-gate: rejected System/Agentic because behavior text is specifically about "content passed to the summary-generation step," i.e., pre-generation payload composition.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real aggregation/orchestration modules with three fake source adapters returning distinct sentinel fields; execute one report run and assert the exact payload handed to summary generation contains all three source sections and key fields.

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a `data unavailable` note for that section rather than aborting.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears when source error handling, report assembly, and continuation logic interact. Unit-only tests of one exception handler miss end-to-end section substitution and non-abort behavior. Integration-brake check: (a) no real network/OS primitive is necessary; a source adapter fake that raises timeout/unavailable is enough, so not System. (b) correctness is fallback/orchestration, not model judgment quality, so not Agentic. (c) no chained agentic steps, so not Workflow. Rejection-gate: rejected System because behavior text says "one data source is unavailable" and expected outcome is fallback note + continue, which can be reproduced with in-process failure injection.
behaviors.test_description: Run orchestrator with two healthy source fakes and one failing fake; verify report object/output contains a `data unavailable` marker only for the failed section and pipeline still completes to the delivery stage interface.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data.
behaviors.justification: This is output-quality correctness of generated narrative vs source facts. Deterministic stubs can only test plumbing, not whether real model generation preserves key values. If run repeatedly with real model, outputs may vary; factual fidelity must be validated against real generations. Rejection-gate: rejected Integration because behavior text requires the summary to "accurately represent" values, which is model-decision quality, not just routing/retries/payload formatting. Rejected Workflow because only one agentic decision point (summary generation) is required by the behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Use fixed structured input with known key metrics/issues/deployments; call the real summary generator/model and evaluate factual consistency (key numbers/entities/events preserved, no contradictions) with a strict rubric over multiple runs and a pass threshold.

behaviors.description: A complete report — from data collection through email delivery — reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary includes real external interfaces: source APIs and email delivery/inbox behavior across network and service boundaries. In-process mocks can hide real auth, transport, formatting, or deliverability failures. Rejection-gate: rejected Integration because behavior explicitly requires "reaches ... inbox correctly," which depends on real email infrastructure behavior. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because the assertion here is delivery success of the pipeline, not model output quality or multi-agent reasoning quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run the script in an environment hitting real test instances of metrics/issue/deploy sources and real email delivery to a test mailbox; assert report email is received by configured recipients with expected subject/body sections and attachment/format if applicable.

behaviors.description: When the email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: Core failure is cross-module state handoff/order: delivery failure -> local persistence -> subsequent run loads pending report -> retry send. This is exactly integration-level orchestration/state propagation (IDEA-PROBE applies). Integration-brake check: (a) real network outage is not strictly required; deterministic failing sender adapter reproduces unreachable condition while exercising real persistence/retry logic, so System is not minimum. (b) no AI/ML output dependence, so not Agentic. (c) no multi-agent chain, so not Workflow. Rejection-gate: rejected System because behavior text centers on fallback persistence and next-run retry semantics, which are reproducible with injected sender failure plus real local filesystem.
behaviors.test_description: Use real retry/persistence modules with temp filesystem and a sender test double that fails first run and succeeds second; run scheduled job logic twice, asserting fallback file is written after first failure, consumed on second run, and email send is retried/succeeds.
